summary: Ukrainian drones attacked a Moscow oil refinery and other targets, prompting Russian air defenses to down 10 UAVs over Moscow. Tactical aviation activity was reported on eastern and northeastern fronts, and three Tu‑95 bombers were relocated from Engels to Olyenya. A Su‑24M crash in Khmelnytskyi resulted in two crew deaths.
key_points: Ukrainian drones struck a refinery in Moscow region, causing emergency pressure reduction (Source: @UkraineNow/81981).
Russian air defenses shot down ten UAVs heading to Moscow (Source: @tass_agency/381003).
Three Tu‑95 strategic bombers were moved from Engels airfield to Olyenya, with possible additional two bombers relocating (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67824).
A Su‑24M of the 7th Tactical Aviation Brigade crashed near Khmelnytskyi, killing both crew members (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67825).
Ukrainian UAV activity was reported over Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, Sumy, Chernihiv and other regions (Source: @kpszsu/65385, @kpszsu/65380, @kpszsu/65403, @kpszsu/65401, @eRadarrua/96938).
Russian oil exports averaged 3.83 million barrels per day in mid‑June, up from earlier in the year (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30951).
Damage to the Kyiv Pechersk Lavra exceeds 500 million hryvnias, with restoration expected to take about two years (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30949).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian drones attacked a Moscow oil refinery, prompting Russian air defenses to down ten UAVs over the capital. Tactical aviation activity intensified on the eastern and northeastern fronts, and three Tu‑95 strategic bombers were relocated from Engels to Olyenya. A Su‑24M crash in Khmelnytskyi killed both crew members.

points: Ukrainian drones were observed over Dnipropetrovsk region near Pavlohrad, Synelnykove, Slavihorod and northwest of Nikopol (Source: @kpszsu/65385).
A reactive UAV was reported over Zaporizhzhia, heading eastward toward the city (Source: @kpszsu/65380).
UAV activity was noted in Dnipropetrovsk heading toward Krynychok (Source: @kpszsu/65403).
In Sumy region, a UAV approached Trostyanets from the northeast (Source: @kpszsu/65401).
In Zaporizhzhia, a UAV moved toward Vilnyansky from the east (Source: @kpszsu/65401).
Chernihiv region reported three UAVs moving from Novgorod toward Siversky (Source: @eRadarrua/96938).
A UAV from Chernihiv was heading toward the Kyiv water reservoir (Source: @kpszsu/65348).
Russian air defense forces shot down ten UAVs heading for Moscow (Source: @tass_agency/381003).
Three Tu‑95MS strategic bombers were relocated from Engels airfield to Olyenya, with reports of two additional bombers moving (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67824).
Russian oil exports reached an average of 3.83 million barrels per day for the period ending 14 June, the highest since the start of the conflict (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30951).
Damage to the Kyiv Pechersk Lavra was assessed at over 500 million hryvnias, with a two‑year restoration timeline (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30949).
A Su‑24M of the 7th Tactical Aviation Brigade crashed near Khmelnytskyi, killing both crew members (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67825).
Russian tactical aviation activity was warned on the eastern and north‑eastern directions, indicating potential strikes on front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/65391, @kpszsu/65384).
Russian forces reportedly intensified pressure around Kostyantynivka, with infantry advancing from multiple directions (Source: @DeepStateUA/23581).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported in Dnipropetrovsk heading toward Krynychok, indicating activity near the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/65403).
Russian tactical aviation activity was flagged on the eastern direction, suggesting possible air strikes on front‑line zones (Source: @kpszsu/65391).
Kostyantynivka faced heavy infantry pressure from the east via Novodmytrivka, with Russian forces attempting to cut supply lines (Source: @DeepStateUA/23581).
Ukrainian UAVs over Zaporizhzhia moved eastward toward the city, potentially targeting logistics nodes (Source: @kpszsu/65380).
Reports indicated Russian forces advancing toward the northern part of Kostyantynivka to encircle the town (Source: @DeepStateUA/23581).
Ukrainian UAV sightings near Sumy and Siverskyi suggest reconnaissance along the northeastern sector (Source: @eRadarrua/96938).
Russian air defenses downed UAVs over Moscow, reflecting broader air‑defense engagements that may affect eastern front air operations (Source: @tass_agency/381003).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over Chernihiv heading toward the Kyiv water reservoir, indicating northern reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/65348).
Russian tactical aviation activity was warned on the north‑eastern direction, with possible strikes on front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/65384).
UAV activity reported in Chernihiv region moving from Novgorod toward Siversky (Source: @eRadarrua/96938).
UAV sightings in Dnipropetrovsk near Yurivka with a western course suggest movements near the northern edge of the front (Source: @kpszsu/65378).
Russian forces were reported pressing around Kostyantynivka, which lies north of the Donetsk axis (Source: @DeepStateUA/23581).
No explicit combat engagements were detailed for the northern front beyond UAV observations and air‑activity warnings.
The relocation of Tu‑95 bombers from Engels may affect northern strategic strike capabilities (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67824).
Ukrainian drones attacked a fuel depot in Poltava settlement on the Kuban, a key logistics hub for Lukoil (Source: @UkraineNow/81956).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported over Dnipropetrovsk heading south of Synelnykove toward Solone/Aviatorske (Source: @eRadarrua/96958).
Russian oil export increases suggest heightened reliance on southern pipeline routes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30951).
No direct combat reports were provided for the southern front beyond the drone strike on the Poltava fuel depot.
Kostyantynivka pressure may influence southern logistics corridors toward Kramatorsk (Source: @DeepStateUA/23581).
Ukrainian UAV activity in Zaporizhzhia heading east could affect southern industrial areas (Source: @kpszsu/65380).
The Su‑24M crash occurred in Khmelnytskyi, which lies in western Ukraine, not directly on the southern front.
Russian air defenses downed ten UAVs targeting Moscow (Source: @tass_agency/381003).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported over multiple regions including Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, Sumy, Chernihiv, and Kyiv reservoir (Source: multiple @kpszsu and @eRadarrua posts).
Warnings of increased Russian tactical aviation activity on eastern and north‑eastern directions were issued (Source: @kpszsu/65391, @kpszsu/65384).
Three Tu‑95MS strategic bombers were relocated, indicating repositioning of long‑range strike assets (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67824).
A Su‑24M crash in Khmelnytskyi resulted in loss of crew, highlighting operational risks for Russian tactical aviation (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67825).
Ukrainian drones attacked a Moscow refinery, demonstrating capability to strike deep inside Russian territory (Source: @UkraineNow/81981).
No explicit air‑to‑air engagements were reported beyond UAV interceptions.

key_developments: Ukrainian drones struck a Moscow oil refinery, prompting emergency response and highlighting deep‑strike capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81981).
Russian air defenses shot down ten UAVs heading for Moscow, showing active air‑defense posture (Source: @tass_agency/381003).
Three Tu‑95MS strategic bombers were relocated from Engels to Olyenya, indicating strategic asset repositioning (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67824).
A Su‑24M of the 7th Tactical Aviation Brigade crashed near Khmelnytskyi, killing both crew members (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67825).

Ukrainian UAV activity was reported across Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, Sumy, Chernihiv and towards Kyiv water reservoir, suggesting widespread reconnaissance and strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/65385, @kpszsu/65380, @kpszsu/65403, @kpszsu/65401, @eRadarrua/96938, @kpszsu/65348).
Russian oil exports rose to an average of 3.83 million barrels per day in mid‑June, reflecting increased reliance on crude shipments (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30951).
Damage to Kyiv Pechersk Lavra exceeds 500 million hryvnias with a two‑year restoration estimate, indicating significant cultural loss (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30949).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV operations demonstrate an expanding strike envelope, reaching deep into Russian territory and targeting critical infrastructure such as oil refineries. Russian tactical aviation activity warnings on eastern and north‑eastern fronts suggest preparation for intensified air operations, while the relocation of Tu‑95 strategic bombers may enhance long‑range strike capabilities. The downing of ten UAVs over Moscow indicates robust air‑defense readiness, yet the successful drone attacks reveal gaps that could be exploited for further high‑value strikes.
sections.regional_response: Russian authorities have intensified air‑defense measures around Moscow, as evidenced by the interception of multiple UAVs. The relocation of strategic bombers from Engels to Olyenya may be aimed at dispersing assets to mitigate vulnerability. Ukrainian forces continue to leverage UAVs for reconnaissance and strike missions across multiple fronts, indicating a coordinated effort to pressure Russian logistics and industrial sites.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths should focus on corridors from Dnipropetrovsk and Zaporizhzhia toward central Russia, as these have yielded successful strikes. The movement of Tu‑95 bombers warrants surveillance of Olyenya airfield for potential launch activity. Continued assessment of Russian air‑defense effectiveness is needed, given the mixed outcomes of UAV interceptions and successful attacks.
